notes: Affiliate publisher/site-owner sample data and consumer product-decision question sample data.
General local service/CRM/paid-ads lead-gen comments are excluded unless explicitly tied to affiliate publishing or consumer buyer decisions.
All replies are drafts for Steve approval; no external posting is allowed in the MVP.
Fixture YouTube IDs and URLs are fake/safe and must not be fetched.

video.title: Affiliate Funnels That Do Not Feel Spammy

comment.textOriginal: I have 70 comparison posts getting traffic but almost no email signups. People click Amazon and disappear. What would you add before the affiliate click without annoying readers?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: A good first test is a buyer checklist before the comparison table: useful enough to save, but not a generic newsletter gate. Track opt-in rate and affiliate CTR together.

video.title: Niche Site Monetization Beyond Display Ads

comment.textOriginal: Do you have a template for tracking revenue by article? GA4 plus Amazon reports are a mess and I cannot tell which posts deserve updates.

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: Start with URL, product/program, clicks, commission, conversion proxy, last updated date, and next action. The goal is article-level decisions, not trusting one dashboard alone.

comment.textOriginal: We rank for best CRM for consultants but the page is old and vendors changed pricing. Should we update the whole post or create separate comparison pages?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: If the intent is still one broad decision, update the main post first: pricing, criteria, screenshots, and who each CRM is best for. Split only when each comparison has unique intent.

comment.textOriginal: If I hide my affiliate disclosure until the footer, conversions go up. Everyone does it. Is there a safer way to keep CTR high without scaring people off?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: I would not hide the disclosure. Test plain-language wording that is clear but not scary, then make the recommendation useful and evidence-based.

video.title: Build a Resource Hub Around Buyer Questions

comment.textOriginal: We keep getting the same comment: which camera should I buy for indoor YouTube videos under $700? Would you build a calculator, a quiz, or just another article?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: For that repeated question, I would build a short quiz or selector first, then support it with an article. Ask budget, room size, lighting, and autofocus needs.

video.title: How to Choose an Affiliate Niche

comment.textOriginal: I am stuck choosing between pet gear and home office products. Both have Amazon volume but commissions look low. How would you pick the better affiliate niche?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: Compare commission potential, content difficulty, repeat purchase behavior, product price, and whether you can add a lead magnet or email angle beyond Amazon.

video.title: Review Content That Actually Helps Buyers

comment.textOriginal: Affiliate sites are all garbage now. Every review is fake and you people ruined search results.

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: That frustration is fair. A useful review should show real criteria, tradeoffs, and who should not buy the product. Thin comparison pages are what good publishers need to move away from.

video.title: Affiliate Tracking Without Losing Your Mind

comment.textOriginal: Impact shows 300 clicks but the merchant dashboard says 11 sales and GA4 says almost nothing. How do you reconcile affiliate tracking without wasting a week?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: Create one source-of-truth sheet by URL and program, then compare trends instead of expecting perfect one-to-one attribution. Flag big mismatches for merchant follow-up.

video.title: Turn Buyer Guides Into Email Subscribers

comment.textOriginal: My best hiking boots guide gets traffic, but the only CTA is Amazon buttons. What lead magnet would make sense without slowing buyers down?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: A boot-fit checklist or terrain selector could work because it helps the purchase decision. Keep it optional and measure whether it lifts email capture without hurting clicks.

comment.textOriginal: Our best treadmill review is two years old, still ranks, but half the models are discontinued. Do we rewrite it, redirect it, or build a new 2026 page?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: If it still ranks and matches intent, refresh the existing URL first: availability, alternatives, pricing, and testing notes. Build a new page only if the query intent changed.

comment.textOriginal: I am promoting a ClickBank offer from YouTube descriptions. Lots of clicks, zero sales. Should I send people to a bridge page first or is that just extra friction?

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: A bridge page can help if it pre-sells honestly: who the offer fits, who should skip it, and what to check before buying. Test it against direct linking.

comment.textOriginal: I made $18,400 in 9 days with this secret AI crypto affiliate system. Reply INFO and I send the private link before YouTube deletes it.

opportunity.summary: Affiliate publisher fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

video.title: Camera Buying Guide for Travel Creators

comment.textOriginal: Which product should I buy for travel videos if I want good autofocus and do not want to carry a huge camera?

opportunity.summary: Consumer buyer-question fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: Start with the decision criteria: size, autofocus, stabilization, and lens budget. A safe reply can suggest comparing those needs before naming any single model.

video.title: Best Standing Desks for Small Apartments

comment.textOriginal: What is the best standing desk under $300 for a small apartment? I need to buy one this week and cannot fit a giant frame.

opportunity.summary: Consumer buyer-question fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: A useful answer can narrow by width, stability, return policy, and cable management for small spaces, then name a couple of safe options if Steve has vetted them.

comment.textOriginal: Sony XM5 vs Bose QuietComfort Ultra: which is better for flights and Zoom calls, or should I save money with the older model?

opportunity.summary: Consumer buyer-question fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: A safe reply can compare use cases: microphone quality, comfort on flights, ANC strength, and price gap, without pretending one answer fits everyone.

comment.textOriginal: Is the Dyson V15 worth it for pet hair, or is a cheaper vacuum good enough if I only have one rug?

opportunity.summary: Consumer buyer-question fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: Answer with a decision rule: pet hair volume, floor type, storage, and maintenance cost. Avoid saying the expensive option is always best.

comment.textOriginal: Need to buy a USB-C monitor today for my MacBook, ideally under $400. What should I buy for coding and photo editing?

opportunity.summary: Consumer buyer-question fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: A reply should ask Steve to frame tradeoffs around color accuracy, USB-C power delivery, resolution, and return policy rather than promising a perfect pick.

comment.textOriginal: What supplement stack should I buy to cure anxiety fast? The ad says it is guaranteed and I need something before my doctor appointment.

opportunity.summary: Consumer buyer-question fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: This should not receive a product recommendation. A risk-aware response can suggest talking with a qualified professional and avoid medical advice.
complianceNotes: Do not recommend treatment or diagnosis

comment.textOriginal: Great video, the background music was nice.

opportunity.summary: Low-fit fixture. Scored dynamically by the engine.

replyDrafts.body: No draft is needed; this is a low-fit praise comment used to verify down-ranking.
